items.evidence: Independent: `python3 -m ruff check scripts/lib/universe.py scripts/lib/value_semantics.py` → exit 0, 'All checks passed!'. Pack: ruff.exit=EXIT:0, ruff.log matches.
items.notes: Scoped to altered modules only; not full-repo ruff (CI does full scripts/ separately).

items.dod_text: §13.4 mypy critical path defined and green
items.evidence: Path defined in mypy-critical-path.txt (universe.py, value_semantics.py, states.py). Independent project mypy exit 0; pack mypy-critical.exit=EXIT:0. Adversarial strict config (disallow_untyped_defs, no scripts.lib ignore_errors) also exit 0 on the same 3 files.
items.notes: CONCERNS residual: pyproject.toml sets ignore_errors=true for scripts.lib.* — project-config green for 2/3 path files is structurally soft. states.py is NOT ignored and is green. MANIFEST correctly disclaims full-repo mypy (mypy.log shows 76 errors / 14 files). Do NOT flip any full-repo mypy claim.

items.evidence: Independent: `python3 -m pip_audit --strict -r requirements.txt` → exit 0, 'No known vulnerabilities found'. Pack: pip-audit.exit=EXIT:0, pip-audit.log matches message.
items.notes: CI uses pip-audit --strict; independent re-run with --strict also green.

items.dod_text: §13.4 no || true in required CI gates
items.evidence: Scan of .github/workflows/ci.yml: soft_fail_code_hits=0 for `|| true`; continue-on-error only in comments. Pack gate-patterns.txt: hits [] count 0.
items.notes: set +e appears once (lines 332-336) to capture expected health --env development exit 2, then `test "$code" -eq 2` fail-closes if not 2. Not a soft-fail of a required gate.

items.evidence: pytest.ini markers: integration, e2e, database, slow. Independent: `pytest -m "integration or e2e" --collect-only -q -o addopts=` → 106/2020 collected, exit 0. Pack external-markers.txt documents on-demand invocation.
items.notes: Claim is runnable on demand, not that live suite is green without DB/network. test_e2e_external.py marked e2e+integration+slow; default addopts excludes slow — intentional on-demand path.

items.dod_text: §13.4 coverage minimum defined for critical paths
items.evidence: .coveragerc [coverage_gate] modules=8 paths (opportunity_intel, coverage, contract_intel, pipeline, universe.py, supplier_metrics.py, price_pipeline.py, reports), threshold=80, output path set. scripts/coverage_gate.py enforces fail-closed (exit 2 below threshold, exit 3 missing .coverage). Pack coverage-gate.txt mirrors config.
items.notes: DEFINED only — not ACHIEVED. Independent coverage_gate run reports .coverage missing (exit path for unmeasured). MANIFEST non-claim accepted. CI cov-fail-under=10 is a separate weaker gate, not the 80% module gate.

items.dod_text: §13.4 critical code without tests has justification registered
items.evidence: docs/ops/session-2026-07-17-full-suite-debt/MANIFEST.md + 02-critical-skip-reasons.txt (copied into campaign pack): 24 skips on critical path all attributed to sc_compras API refactoring removal; open debts listed (load_target_universe, schema views needing REQUIRE_TEST_DB, test-all workflow_dispatch only).
items.notes: Registry is skip/debt documentation, not a formal inventory of every untested critical module. Altered modules in this slice (universe, value_semantics) DO have unit tests. Acceptable for DoD wording as justification registered.

items.evidence: scripts/lib/universe.py:normalize_codigo_ibge (7-digit IBGE). tests/test_universe.py: test_normalize_codigo_ibge_valid + test_normalize_codigo_ibge_invalid. Independent pytest pack: 20 passed exit 0 (includes these).
items.notes: Unit coverage of happy path, punctuation strip, None/empty/wrong-length/non-digit. Not a live IBGE API integration claim.

items.evidence: scripts/lib/value_semantics.py (ValorSemantica, SOURCE_VALUE_TYPES, calculate_desagio, compute_bid_contract_desagio, aggregate_contract_values). tests/test_value_semantics.py: 7 tests all passing in independent re-run.
items.notes: Asserts PNCP bids=ESTIMADO / contracts=CONTRATADO (not PAGO/GLOBAL), deságio math, aggregation. Fixture/unit only — not live edital→contrato chain (MANIFEST non-claim respected).

reproduced_commands.notes: Adversarial strict config without scripts.lib ignore_errors
Tool loads threshold=80 / 8 modules; fails closed without .coverage (not run as measured gate)

risks.description: pyproject.toml [[tool.mypy.overrides]] ignore_errors=true for scripts.lib.* makes project-config mypy green on universe/value_semantics partially vacuous. Adversarial probe: intentionally untyped scripts/lib module still exits 0 under project config; fails under strict config.
risks.recommendation: Remove scripts.lib.* from ignore_errors (or narrow to ignore_missing_imports only) before treating mypy as a hard type gate for lib modules. Keep critical-path command as regression after that.

risks.description: Evidence hygiene: docs/ops/session-2026-07-18-campaign-q13-4/mypy.exit claims EXIT:0 while mypy.log ends with 'Found 76 errors in 14 files'. Critical-path artifacts (mypy-critical.*) are consistent; the full-path exit file is not.
risks.recommendation: Rewrite mypy.exit to match mypy.log (non-zero) or delete mypy.exit/mypy.log if unused. Steward must not treat mypy.exit as proof of full-repo green.

risks.description: Coverage minimum is defined (80%) but not measured in this pack; CI still uses --cov-fail-under=10 on a narrower suite.
risks.recommendation: Optional follow-up: run pytest --cov + scripts/coverage_gate.py and store report — do not flip any 'coverage achieved' DoD without that.

risks.description: Real-source/e2e tests are collectable on demand but not executed green in this cycle; many require DB/network.
risks.recommendation: Keep claim limited to runnable-on-demand; do not imply live suite green.
